The empty-states literally tell the user to 'Schedule a pasada from a ready plot' and 'Log a bloom' — instructions for controls that do not exist. The defining workflow of the whole farm (uniform-ripeness Geisha picking staggered across 340 m) is non-operable from the UI.
findings.scenario: Owner opens /plan after deploy. Left column: a wall of identical '0% · Developing — weeks early · No bloom logged — date unknown · Low confidence' cards. Right column + 'Passes scheduled: 0': the 'No passes scheduled yet' empty-state telling them to 'Schedule a pasada from a ready plot' — but there is no Schedule button, no Log-a-bloom button, no re-plan affordance anywhere on the page. The feature is a static mock; the picker's morning is never produced.
findings.fix: Add the interactive write surfaces this slice's actions already expect: a client 'Log maturation signal' control (bloom date / GDD / NDVI) on each ReadinessList row calling recordMaturationSignal, and a 'Schedule pasada' + 'Re-plan' control on ready plots / timeline rows calling schedulePasada/replanPasada — all built on the portal-fixed shared Dialog (src/components/ui/dialog.tsx), with pending/error states surfacing PlanResult.error. Also seed plot_phenology + at least one pasada_schedule row so the planner has real data on a fresh install (or the page can never be exercised). Until a control exists, the empty-state copy promising those actions is misleading.

findings.title: Pasada status (planned/dispatched/picked) and firedTaskId are fetched but never rendered — the timeline can't show which passes are done vs upcoming
findings.location: pasada-timeline.tsx:51-104 — the row maps p.predictedReadyDate/plotName/altitude/ripenessTarget/reason but NEVER references p.status or p.firedTaskId; PlotReadiness type + v_pasada_calendar (migration:243-258) both carry status ('planned'|'dispatched'|'picked'|'superseded') and fired_task_id
findings.problem: v_pasada_calendar and mapPasadaPlan deliberately carry status and firedTaskId to the component, but PasadaTimeline renders neither. The 'wave moving up the mountain' shows every active pass with identical styling whether it is still 'planned', already 'dispatched' to the crew, or already 'picked'. For a staggered multi-pasada season — the entire reason this view exists — the user has no way to tell which passes are complete and which are upcoming, and no link from a pass to the task it fired. This is a real feature gap vs the design ('feeds the dispatch board', the closing-pass/rebusca tracking in ROADMAP:318), not a polish nit: the calendar's primary job is to communicate progress through the pick sequence.
findings.scenario: Mid-season, three passes on Cuesta de Piedra are picked, two on the high Geisha are still planned. The timeline shows all five looking identical — no 'picked' check, no dimming, no 'on the board' link — so the owner cannot read the season's progress from the one screen built to show it, and re-derives it by walking /tasks.
findings.fix: Render p.status as a state chip/affordance per row (e.g. picked = forest check + dimmed, dispatched = honey 'on the board', planned = neutral) and, when p.firedTaskId is set, link/indicate the fired task. Add a render test asserting a picked pass is visually distinguished and a fired-task indicator appears. Trivial since the data is already in hand.

findings.title: Readiness list shows a raw, un-localized ISO date string ('Ready ~ 2026-04-01') to a Spanish/Ngäbere-speaking family
findings.location: readiness-list.tsx:108-110 — `{r.predictedReadyDate ? (<>Ready ~ {r.predictedReadyDate}</>)` prints the raw PostgREST date string verbatim
findings.problem: predictedReadyDate arrives from the view as a bare Postgres date serialized as 'YYYY-MM-DD' and is dropped straight into the card with no formatting. The rest of the app formats numbers via num()/usd()/pct() for a non-technical, primarily Spanish/Ngäbe-Buglé audience, but the single most action-driving value on each readiness card — WHEN to pick — is shown as a machine ISO string. It is also ambiguous internationally (2026-04-01 reads as 1 April only if you know ISO) and inconsistent with the timeline, which at least splits the date into MM-DD + year. The world-class bar ('Spanish/Ngäbere', dignity-first) is missed on the card's key field.
findings.scenario: Owner scans the ranked list to plan the week; each card's pick date reads 'Ready ~ 2026-04-08' — a developer date format, not a human one, and not localized for the people reading it.
findings.fix: Format predictedReadyDate through a date helper (e.g. toLocaleDateString with the app's locale, or a short 'Apr 8' / 'lun 8 abr' rendering consistent with the timeline). Add a render test asserting the human-formatted date appears and the raw ISO string does not.

findings.title: Timeline date marker silently breaks if predictedReadyDate is ever not a 10-char ISO string (slice(5)/slice(0,4) with no guard)
findings.location: pasada-timeline.tsx:63-67 — `{p.predictedReadyDate.slice(5)}` (MM-DD) and `{p.predictedReadyDate.slice(0,4)}` (year); ordering at :43-45 also assumes lexical ISO ordering equals chronological
findings.problem: The date marker derives MM-DD and year purely by string slicing predictedReadyDate, and the defensive re-sort uses localeCompare — both correct ONLY while the value is an exact 'YYYY-MM-DD' string. The view (migration:255) returns a Postgres date so today it holds, but there is no NaN/format guard, no render test pins the shape, and any future change that lets the value be null, a full timestamp, or a localized string would render a blank/garbled marker (e.g. slice(5) of a timestamp shows '-04-18T00') and silently mis-sort. This is latent (not reachable on the current view) but the component has no test asserting the marker renders correctly for the contract date shape.
findings.scenario: A later slice swaps the view to emit a timestamptz or a pre-formatted date (a plausible refactor); the timeline's date column quietly renders junk MM-DD with no error, because nothing pins the parse.
findings.fix: Parse predictedReadyDate via a Date/format helper that handles a non-ISO/empty input gracefully (fallback to '—') instead of raw slice(), and add a render test asserting the marker shows the expected month/day for the contract date and degrades cleanly for a malformed value.

findings.title: Reposo gate is bypassed by the DEFAULT pipeline path (drying→parchment→milled) and by drying→green; it only guards the literal drying→milled edge
findings.location: L442 (RPC gate condition) and L477 (trigger backstop condition); interacts with batch_stage enum order in 20260620120000_init.sql L17 and the forward-only-but-no-skip guard at L429
findings.problem: The load-bearing Phase-2 invariant — a lot CANNOT advance out of drying toward milling until rest-stable — is enforced ONLY on the single transition `cur_stage='drying' AND p_to_stage='milled'` (RPC L442; trigger backstop L477). But the canonical pipeline is `drying → parchment → milled → green` (lot_yield_curve seeds `('drying','parchment',0.90),('parchment','milled',0.80)` in 20260621092000 L195-196; processing/actions.ts and advance-stage-control.tsx both document this order). advance_processing_stage has a forward-only guard (L429) but NO single-step / no-skip guard, so ANY forward jump is legal. Two fully-reachable bypass routes evade the gate entirely: (1) `drying → parchment` then `parchment → milled` — and `parchment` is `forward[0]` from drying, i.e. the DEFAULT option the Advance dialog pre-selects (advance-stage-control.tsx forwardStages() + `defaultValue={nextStage}` L186); neither hop matches `drying→milled`. (2) `drying → green` directly — `green` is offered in the forward set from drying and green>drying passes forward-only, while new.stage='green'≠'milled' so the gate never fires (this also skips milled and the green_lots materialization). The BEFORE-UPDATE trigger backstop on lots has the identical condition (L477) so it does not catch either route. Net: a wet, un-rested lot reaches `milled`/`green` through the normal UI flow with zero moisture/rest enforcement — the gate is decorative on every path except the one nobody is forced to take.
findings.scenario: On the live farm, JC-552 (Geisha Anaerobic, physically drying) is in the mill. Operator opens the Advance dialog from /processing or /drying; the dialog defaults the target to 'Parchment' (forward[0]). They click Advance lot → `advance_processing_stage(JC-552,'parchment',…)`: gate condition false (to_stage≠'milled'). Open the dialog again, default is now 'Milled' → `advance_processing_stage(JC-552,'milled',…)`: gate condition false (cur_stage='parchment'≠'drying'). The lot is milled with no moisture-band / min-rest-days check ever evaluated. The reposo gate — 'the cup is defined by the reposo' — has been fully bypassed via two ordinary button clicks.
findings.fix: Make the gate guard the EXIT from drying, not one hard-coded edge. Cleanest: gate the FIRST forward step out of drying — fire whenever `from='drying' AND to>'drying'` (i.e. any advance leaving drying: parchment, milled, or green). Equivalently/additionally, fire whenever the move crosses INTO or PAST milled from any pre-milled stage. Mirror the exact same broadened predicate in BOTH the RPC (L442) and the trigger backstop (L477) so neither path leaks. Add a no-skip (single-step) guard to advance_processing_stage so `drying→green` cannot leapfrog milled in one move, and add a DB-level test that asserts drying→parchment→milled and drying→green are both blocked when not rest-stable.

findings.title: Reposo gate is inert for every real (seeded/legacy) lot because lots.stage is NULL, so the gate condition evaluates to 'cherry' and never fires
findings.location: L442 (RPC `coalesce(nullif(cur_stage,''),'cherry')='drying'`) and L477 (trigger `coalesce(nullif(old.stage,''),'cherry')='drying'`); root cause: lots.stage added nullable with no default/backfill (20260621092000 L77) and seed inserts lots with code only (supabase/seed.sql L67-75)
findings.problem: The gate keys off `lots.stage`. But `lots` was created with only `code` (init L57-59); `stage` is added later as nullable `text` with no default and no backfill (20260621092000 L77). supabase/seed.sql L67-75 inserts the 8 real lots with `insert into lots (code) values (...)` — so lots.stage IS NULL for all of them. The physical drying state lives in processing_batches.stage (seed L115-117: JC-573, JC-564, JC-552 all 'drying'), which the gate never reads and which no migration backfills into lots.stage. The gate's NULL handling treats `coalesce(nullif(cur_stage,''),'cherry')` as 'cherry', so for a NULL-stage lot the condition `…='drying'` is FALSE. Result: a direct `advance_processing_stage(JC-573,'milled',…)` — or even a raw `update lots set stage='milled'` (the trigger has the same NULL inertia at L477) — is NOT gated at all, because the lot reads as 'cherry' not 'drying'. assign_drying_station does NOT set lots.stage either (it only writes drying_assignments), so committing a lot to a drying bed leaves stage NULL and the gate inert. The gate fires only for lots whose lots.stage was driven to the literal 'drying' via the RPC — i.e. NOT the lots actually on the patios today. The bypass FAILS OPEN (gate silently doesn't run), the worst failure mode for an invariant.
findings.scenario: JC-573 (Caturra Washed) is physically drying at 18.5% moisture, started 2026-06-11 (seed L115), but lots.stage is NULL. An operator advances it straight to milled: `advance_processing_stage('JC-573','milled',420,…)`. cur_stage=NULL → treated as 'cherry' → forward-only allows cherry→milled, no-mass-gain passes, and the gate condition (`'cherry'='drying'`) is false → the lot mills at 18.5% with zero days of certified rest. The trigger backstop is equally inert (old.stage NULL → 'cherry'). The reposo invariant is bypassed for exactly the lots it exists to protect.
findings.fix: Backfill lots.stage from processing_batches.stage (and going forward keep it authoritative) so the spine's stage reflects physical reality — e.g. in this migration `update lots l set stage = pb.stage from (select distinct on (lot_code) lot_code, stage from processing_batches order by lot_code, started_date desc) pb where pb.lot_code=l.code and l.stage is null`. Then change the gate so a NULL/unknown source stage on a drying-or-later move does NOT silently disable it: the gate should fire on the drying-exit predicate computed from the lot's REAL stage, and a lot with no determinable stage that is being moved to milled/green should fail CLOSED (require an explicit rest-stable verdict) rather than fall through as 'cherry'. Add a test seeding a NULL-stage drying lot and asserting the advance-to-milled is blocked.

findings.title: Reposo-gate rejection surfaces as a raw labelled error, not a family-readable message (no friendlyRpcError mapping)
findings.dimension: MIGRATION/SCHEMA-adjacent (RPC error contract vs command mapper)
findings.location: friendlyRpcError() L161-187 — no branch matches the gate's `'reposo gate: lot % not rest-stable (%)'` message (raised at migration L445/L480)
findings.problem: When the reposo gate DOES fire (a legitimately-staged lot on a direct drying→milled move), advance_processing_stage raises `check_violation` with message `reposo gate: lot JC-561 not rest-stable (resting 2/5 days)`. friendlyRpcError matches backward / mass-gain / FK / unique / bad-enum, but has NO branch for 'reposo gate'. Worse, the message contains no digits-only token but the FIRST branch keys on code 23503 (FK) only — the reposo raise uses code 'check_violation' (23514-class) and the message does not match `/backward/`, `/cannot increase/`, or `/batch_stage/`, so friendlyRpcError returns null and the command falls through to `advance_processing_stage: reposo gate: lot JC-561 not rest-stable (resting 2/5 days)`. The family sees a raw, prefixed engine string in the on-brand alert — exactly the 'never a raw Postgres exception' contract this layer exists to uphold. (This is the gate's ONLY user-facing feedback; given the two bypasses above it rarely fires, but when it does the UX is broken.)
findings.scenario: After the two CRITs are fixed and the gate actually fires, an operator tries to mill JC-561 at day 2 of 5. The dialog shows: 'advance_processing_stage: reposo gate: lot JC-561 not rest-stable (resting 2/5 days)' — engine text with a function-name prefix — instead of a clean 'Lot JC-561 is still resting (2 of 5 days). It can't go to the mill until it's rest-stable.'
findings.fix: Add a branch to friendlyRpcError matching `/reposo gate|rest-stable|not yet stable/i` (and/or check the raised reason text) returning a clean message that echoes st.reason, e.g. `"Lot ${lotCode} isn't rest-stable yet — ${reasonTail}. It can't go to the mill until it's rested and its moisture is in band."`. Pin it with a command test feeding the gate's exact error message and asserting a friendly, exception-free result (test-first).

findings.title: Fired 'Harvest' task crashes the /tasks board — DB enum extended, TS TaskCategory contract not (undefined icon → render throw)
findings.location: migration:49 (alter type task_category add value 'Harvest'); migration:361-372 & 458-467 (schedule_pasada/replan_pasada insert category='Harvest'); src/lib/types.ts:92-99 (TaskCategory union, no 'Harvest'); src/components/sections/tasks/task-board.tsx:31-39,61,68 (CATEGORY_ICON Record + <Icon/>)
findings.problem: The slice extends the shared phase-1 `task_category` enum with `'Harvest'` and `schedule_pasada`/`replan_pasada` insert real rows into the phase-1 `tasks` table with `category = 'Harvest'`. But the TS contract `TaskCategory` (src/lib/types.ts:92-99 — an explicit CLAUDE.md 'don't fork' contract) was NOT extended, and the /tasks board consumes it via exhaustive `Record<TaskCategory, IconType>`/`Record<TaskCategory, BadgeTone>` lookups with no fallback. `TaskBoard` (rendered on /tasks) calls `getTasks()` → reads `tasks_view` → returns the fired 'Harvest' task (status 'todo' → lands in the kanban). At task-board.tsx:61 `const Icon = CATEGORY_ICON['Harvest']` is `undefined`; rendering `<Icon className=.../>` at :68 throws. REPRODUCED: a render test mounting TaskBoard with a category:'Harvest' task throws `Error: Element type is invalid... got: undefined. Check the render method of TaskTile.` → the entire /tasks route 500s. This is the data-layer change (a new enum value the consuming contract can't represent) breaking a phase-1 surface; reachable the moment anyone runs schedule_pasada (the slice's own DB test fires exactly such a task). The migration header even FLAGS 'this is the one place the slice extends a shared phase-1 domain type' but the cross-layer coherence was never closed.
findings.scenario: Family schedules a pasada in /plan (or any authenticated user calls schedule_pasada). One Harvest task lands on the board. Next visit to /tasks: TaskBoard renders the todo column, hits the undefined icon, throws → /tasks page is dead for everyone until the row is deleted. (TaskTable degrades — Badge default-param swallows undefined tone — but TaskBoard renders first and crashes the route.)
findings.fix: Add 'Harvest' to the TaskCategory union in src/lib/types.ts AND give it a tone+icon in BOTH CATEGORY_ICON/CATEGORY_TONE maps in task-board.tsx and task-table.tsx (e.g. a Coffee/Cherry lucide icon, 'cherry' or 'forest' tone). Belt-and-suspenders: make the icon lookup total with a fallback (`const Icon = CATEGORY_ICON[task.category] ?? HelpCircle`) so a future enum add can't 500 the board. Ship a render test mounting TaskBoard with a category:'Harvest' task asserting no throw (the slice's DB test fires the task but never asserts the board can render it).

findings.title: schedule_pasada has no single-active guard — duplicate active plans per (plot, pasada) and duplicate fired tasks
findings.location: pasada_schedule table 112-133 (only uniques are idempotency_key + (device_id,device_seq); pasada_schedule_active_idx:133 is a NON-unique partial index); schedule_pasada 340-407 (never checks for an existing active plan); v_pasada_calendar 243-259 (filters only status <> 'superseded')
findings.problem: There is NO constraint enforcing 'at most one ACTIVE (non-superseded) plan per (plot_id, pasada_number)'. The idempotency guard is per idempotency_key only. So calling schedule_pasada twice for the same (plot, pasada) with two different keys (or schedule_pasada again instead of replan_pasada) appends TWO `status='planned'` rows and fires TWO tasks — both active. `replan_pasada` is the ONLY path that supersedes the prior plan; `schedule_pasada` blindly appends. `v_pasada_calendar` then surfaces BOTH active rows for the same (plot, pasada), so the /plan calendar shows duplicate/conflicting passes and the picker can get two Harvest tasks for the same pass. The slice's own test (s_p2s8_harvest_planning.db.test.ts:288) asserts 'only the ACTIVE plan per (plot, pasada)' returns exactly 1 — but that invariant is upheld only by test discipline (the test happens to use replan_pasada), NOT by the data layer. An append-only/superseded design whose 'one active per key' invariant lives only in caller convention is bypassable.
findings.scenario: Two devices offline-replay a pasada for plot p-x pasada 1 (distinct device_id/seq → distinct idempotency keys), or the family re-schedules via schedule_pasada instead of replan_pasada. Two active plans + two 'pick p-x' tasks now exist; /plan shows the pass twice and two pickers may both head to the same plot for pass 1.
findings.fix: Add a partial UNIQUE index enforcing single-active per pass: `create unique index pasada_one_active_idx on pasada_schedule (plot_id, pasada_number) where status <> 'superseded';`. Then have schedule_pasada either error (or auto-supersede like replan_pasada) when an active plan already exists for (plot, pasada). Add a DB test that calls schedule_pasada twice (distinct keys) for the same pass and asserts the second is rejected / supersedes the first.

findings.title: pasada_schedule.fired_task_id is a dangling text reference into a freely DELETE-able tasks table — broken plan→task audit chain
findings.location: pasada_schedule.fired_task_id text 124 (no FK, no index); write_foundation.sql:67-81 grants INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on `tasks` to authenticated + permissive insert/update/delete RLS with check(true)
findings.problem: The slice's whole narrative is 'RPC-only write door, append-only history.' But the fired deliverable — the `tasks` row — lands in a phase-1 table that ANY authenticated user can freely UPDATE or DELETE (write_foundation.sql:67-81: direct DML grants + `with check (true)` RLS). pasada_schedule.fired_task_id is plain text with NO foreign key to tasks(id), so: (a) deleting the fired task leaves fired_task_id pointing at nothing — the auditable 'this plan fired that task' link silently rots, with no referential guard; (b) the append-only pasada_schedule (immutable by trigger) can end up referencing a task that's been edited to a different plot/worker/category, desyncing the snapshot. This is an S8-introduced coupling (the plan claims to own a task it cannot protect). It does not corrupt the new tables, hence MED, but it undercuts the slice's stated immutable-audit guarantee.
findings.scenario: Pasada plan fires task T. A user opens /tasks, deletes T (allowed — direct DELETE grant). pasada_schedule still shows fired_task_id=T's id; the calendar/audit trail claims a task that no longer exists, with nothing flagging the break.
findings.fix: Either add `fired_task_id text references tasks(id) on delete set null` (so a deleted task nulls the link visibly) plus an index, or document that tasks are mutable phase-1 rows and surface fired-task liveness in v_pasada_calendar (left join tasks, expose a 'task_missing' flag). At minimum index fired_task_id if it's ever looked up.

findings.title: _resolve_pasada_worker returns NULL when zero workers exist → schedule_pasada/replan_pasada fail the worker_id NOT NULL with an opaque error
findings.location: _resolve_pasada_worker 267-293 (returns text, limit 1 over a UNION that is empty when no workers/harvests); schedule_pasada insert into tasks 361-372 uses the result as worker_id (tasks.worker_id NOT NULL per init.sql:99)
findings.problem: The resolver's comment promises it 'NEVER returns null when any worker exists', but if the `workers` table is empty (and the plot has no harvests) all three UNION branches are empty → it returns NULL. schedule_pasada/replan_pasada then insert NULL into tasks.worker_id (NOT NULL) and abort with a raw not-null_violation rather than a domain message, and the whole pasada-scheduling txn rolls back. For this farm with a standing crew it's an edge, but it's an unhandled invariant path (the function asserts a guarantee it doesn't enforce) and the failure surfaces as an opaque constraint error to the offline replay/outbox.
findings.scenario: A freshly-reset/empty demo DB (or a tenant before any worker is seeded) calls schedule_pasada → tasks insert hits worker_id NOT NULL → cryptic 23502 error instead of a clear 'no worker available to assign' message; the pasada is silently never scheduled.
findings.fix: In schedule_pasada/replan_pasada, after `worker := _resolve_pasada_worker(...)`, `if worker is null then raise exception 'no worker available to assign the harvest pass' using errcode='no_data_found'; end if;` so the failure is a clear domain error, and add a DB test for the zero-worker path.

findings.title: Missing index on pasada_schedule.superseded_by self-FK (hot on the supersede/lineage path)
findings.location: pasada_schedule.superseded_by bigint references pasada_schedule(id) :123; indexes created at 132-133 cover (plot_id,pasada_number) and partial(status) only
findings.problem: superseded_by is a self-referential FK but has no index. Postgres does not auto-index FK columns, and replan_pasada's supersede-chain plus any 'what superseded this plan / walk the plan lineage' query (the auditable history the slice advertises) scans pasada_schedule. On a small farm it's negligible today (so LOW), but it's the one indexable hot path the slice left uncovered while indexing the others, and an unindexed FK also slows any future delete/validate of the referenced row.
findings.scenario: As seasons accrue replanned passes, a 'show full re-plan lineage for this pass' query (follow superseded_by) does a seq scan; trivially avoidable.
findings.fix: `create index pasada_schedule_superseded_by_idx on pasada_schedule (superseded_by) where superseded_by is not null;`

findings.title: Reposo gate is fully bypassable on the canonical pipeline path (drying→parchment→milled) — the gate predicate guards only the non-canonical direct drying→milled jump
findings.location: L442 (RPC layer-1 gate: `if coalesce(nullif(cur_stage,''),'cherry')='drying' and p_to_stage='milled'`) and L477 (trigger layer-2 backstop: `if ... old.stage='drying' and new.stage='milled'`)
findings.problem: The batch_stage enum is ('cherry','fermentation','drying','parchment','milled','green') — confirmed in 20260620120000_init.sql:17 and the lot_yield_curve in 20260621092000_event_log_units_lot_graph.sql:192-197, which defines the real edges drying→parchment (0.90) and parchment→milled (0.80). The canonical pipeline therefore goes drying → PARCHMENT → milled, with 'parchment' (pergamino — literally the reposo/rest stage) sitting between drying and milled. But BOTH gate layers fire ONLY on the exact transition cur_stage='drying' AND to_stage='milled'. On the real path: drying→parchment is a forward move (parchment > drying), allowed by the forward-only guard and NOT gated; parchment→milled is also forward and NOT gated (cur_stage is 'parchment', not 'drying'). The load-bearing Phase-2 invariant — a lot CANNOT advance to milling until moisture-stable (10.5–11.5%) AND min rest days — is enforced on a path the pipeline never takes, and is silently skipped on the path it always takes. This is a data-layer INVARIANT that can be bypassed (CRIT per the brief). It defeats the entire quality promise the ROADMAP states five times ('a lot CANNOT advance to MILLING until...', docs/ROADMAP.md:81,332,344,353,1359,1375). At Volcán a soaking-wet, un-rested reserve Geisha worth $100+/lb green can be milled with zero gate resistance — exactly the cup-destroying mistake the gate exists to prevent. NOTE: the live UI makes this the DEFAULT one-click path — src/components/sections/processing/advance-stage-control.tsx:62-63 offers getForwardStages = everything after current and L159 sets nextStage=forward[0]; for a drying lot forward[0] is 'parchment', so the default 'Advance' button does drying→parchment (ungated), then the next default is parchment→milled (ungated). The drying board's own advance-to-mill affordance is a disabled decoration (drying-board.tsx:16-19), so the gate is unreachable on the normal flow. The p2s4 test suite passes only because every test (incl. the 'SUCCEEDS' and trigger-backstop cases, p2s4_drying_reposo.db.test.ts:60-172) drives drying→milled DIRECTLY, skipping parchment — it tests the one path the gate covers and never the real path that bypasses it.
findings.scenario: REPRO (run against the real migrations via the PGlite harness; verified passing, then deleted — also independently reproduced by a second red-team agent's throwaway in the same worktree): mint a cherry lot, advance cherry→fermentation→drying, record ONE moisture reading of 18.0% at now() (v_reposo_status.ready = false, proving it is wet and un-rested). Then `select advance_processing_stage(lot,'parchment',...)` succeeds (forward-only OK, ungated), then `select advance_processing_stage(lot,'milled',...)` succeeds (cur_stage='parchment', gate predicate false) — lot.stage is now 'milled' with NO reposo raise. A direct `update lots set stage='parchment'` then `update lots set stage='milled'` evades the trigger backstop identically (the trigger's WHEN never matches old.stage='parchment'→'milled' nor 'drying'→'parchment'). reposo_status said NOT ready throughout; the wet, zero-rest lot is milled anyway.
findings.fix: Gate the boundary the pipeline actually crosses, not a non-canonical jump. The reposo/rest happens IN parchment, so the milling boundary is the exit from parchment. Change both layers to fire when crossing INTO milled from the resting stages: in advance_processing_stage (L442) use `if coalesce(nullif(cur_stage,''),'cherry') in ('drying','parchment') and p_to_stage='milled'` (and likewise gate drying→parchment if parchment entry should also require stability), and in lots_enforce_reposo_gate (L477) mirror it: `if old.stage in ('drying','parchment') and new.stage='milled'`. Better: gate ANY transition whose target is 'milled' (or beyond) from a pre-milled stage, since the invariant is 'no milling until rest-stable' regardless of the immediate predecessor. Then ADD a test that drives the CANONICAL drying→parchment→milled path on a wet/un-rested lot and asserts it RAISES (the missing regression net), plus a green-path test through parchment. Also align advance-stage-control.tsx so the milling step is only offered/enabled when v_reposo_status.ready is true for the lot.

findings.title: Reposo rest-clock is computed from a fully client-controlled timestamp (assign_drying_station.p_occurred_at → assigned_at), so min-rest-days can be satisfied by back-dating an assignment
findings.location: reposo_status L230-244 (`select min(a.assigned_at) into started ... rest_days := extract(epoch from (now()-started))/86400.0`) fed by assign_drying_station L347/L373/L377 where assigned_at := p_occurred_at, with NO `p_occurred_at <= now()` / sanity bound anywhere in the RPC or the command validator
findings.problem: The 'rest met' half of the gate (r_met := rest_days >= min_reposo_days) derives entirely from started = min(assigned_at) (falling back to the first drying stage_advance occurred_at, also client-supplied). assign_drying_station accepts p_occurred_at verbatim and writes it as assigned_at; neither the RPC, the trigger prevent_overcapacity, nor the validator in src/lib/db/commands/assignStation.ts bounds it to <= now(). So calling assign_drying_station(lot, station, now() - interval '60 days') instantly makes rest_days≈60 and r_met true even on a lot that started drying today. Combined with three in-band readings, ready becomes true and the gate opens with no real reposo. Because moisture readings' occurred_at is likewise unbounded (record_moisture_reading L327, validator only checks ISO-shape, not <= now), the moisture-stability half can be staged the same way. In this single-owner authenticated app this is the owner self-defeating their own quality gate rather than a tenant/privilege breach, so it is below CRIT — but it turns a 'data-layer guarantee' the ROADMAP markets to buyers/auditors ('enforced at the data layer, not on trust') back into an honor system on a client clock, undermining the tamper-evidence claim the drying dossier is sold on.
findings.scenario: Owner under mill-yard time pressure calls assign_drying_station('JC-602','st-bed-1', now() - interval '7 days') on a lot that actually landed on the bed today, records a couple of conveniently in-band readings, and the gate reports ready — milling a lot that never rested. The append-only ledger now carries a back-dated assigned_at as 'evidence', so the dossier looks compliant.
findings.fix: Bound the field clock in the write door: in assign_drying_station and record_moisture_reading raise check_violation if p_occurred_at > now() + small_skew (and reject absurdly-old values for assignments per business rule), and mirror the same rule in the command validators (assignStation.ts / recordMoisture.ts isISOTimestamp paths) for a friendly pre-roundtrip error. Optionally anchor the rest clock to the server accept clock (recorded_at) rather than the client occurred_at for the gate math, keeping occurred_at for display — so a back-dated field time cannot shorten real elapsed rest.

findings.title: Reposo gate is fully bypassable on the canonical pipeline path (drying→parchment→milled) — the gate predicate guards only the non-canonical direct drying→milled jump
findings.location: L442 (RPC layer-1 gate: cur_stage='drying' and p_to_stage='milled') and L477 (trigger layer-2 backstop: old.stage='drying' and new.stage='milled')
findings.problem: The batch_stage enum is ('cherry','fermentation','drying','parchment','milled','green') (20260620120000_init.sql:17), and lot_yield_curve (20260621092000_event_log_units_lot_graph.sql:192-197) defines the real edges drying->parchment and parchment->milled. The canonical pipeline goes drying -> PARCHMENT -> milled, with 'parchment' (pergamino, literally the reposo/rest stage) between drying and milled. But BOTH gate layers fire ONLY when cur_stage='drying' AND to_stage='milled'. On the real path: drying->parchment is forward (allowed by forward-only) and NOT gated; parchment->milled is forward and NOT gated (cur_stage is 'parchment'). So the load-bearing Phase-2 invariant (no advance to milling until moisture-stable 10.5-11.5% AND min rest days) is enforced only on a path the pipeline never takes and skipped on the path it always takes. A data-layer INVARIANT that can be bypassed = CRIT. It defeats the quality promise the ROADMAP states repeatedly (docs/ROADMAP.md:81,332,344,353,1359,1375). A soaking-wet, un-rested reserve Geisha worth $100+/lb can be milled with zero gate resistance. LIVE REACHABILITY: src/components/sections/processing/advance-stage-control.tsx:62-63 offers getForwardStages (everything after current) and L159 sets nextStage=forward[0]; for a drying lot forward[0] is 'parchment', so the DEFAULT one-click Advance does drying->parchment (ungated), then default parchment->milled (ungated). The drying board's advance-to-mill button is a disabled decoration (drying-board.tsx:16-19), so the gate is unreachable on the normal flow. The p2s4 test suite passes only because every test (p2s4_drying_reposo.db.test.ts:60-172) drives drying->milled DIRECTLY, skipping parchment.
findings.scenario: REPRO (run against the real migrations via the PGlite harness, verified passing, then deleted; also independently reproduced by a second red-team agent's throwaway in this worktree): mint a cherry lot, advance cherry->fermentation->drying, record ONE moisture reading of 18.0% at now() (v_reposo_status.ready=false). Then advance_processing_stage(lot,'parchment',...) succeeds (ungated), then advance_processing_stage(lot,'milled',...) succeeds (cur_stage='parchment', gate predicate false) - lot.stage='milled' with NO reposo raise. Direct `update lots set stage='parchment'` then `'milled'` evades the trigger backstop identically. reposo_status said NOT ready throughout; the wet, zero-rest lot is milled anyway.
findings.fix: Gate the boundary the pipeline actually crosses. The rest happens IN parchment, so the milling boundary is exit from parchment. In advance_processing_stage (L442): `if coalesce(nullif(cur_stage,''),'cherry') in ('drying','parchment') and p_to_stage='milled'`; in lots_enforce_reposo_gate (L477) mirror it: `if old.stage in ('drying','parchment') and new.stage='milled'`. Strongest: gate ANY transition whose target is 'milled' from a pre-milled stage, since the invariant is 'no milling until rest-stable'. Add a regression test driving the CANONICAL drying->parchment->milled path on a wet/un-rested lot and asserting it RAISES, plus a green-path-through-parchment test. Align advance-stage-control.tsx so the milling step is only enabled when v_reposo_status.ready is true.

findings.title: Reposo rest-clock derives from a fully client-controlled timestamp (assign_drying_station.p_occurred_at -> assigned_at), so min-rest-days can be satisfied by back-dating an assignment
findings.location: reposo_status L230-244 (started := min(a.assigned_at); rest_days := extract(epoch from (now()-started))/86400.0) fed by assign_drying_station L347/L373/L377 (assigned_at := p_occurred_at) with NO p_occurred_at <= now() bound in the RPC or src/lib/db/commands/assignStation.ts
findings.problem: The 'rest met' half of the gate (r_met := rest_days >= min_reposo_days) derives entirely from started = min(assigned_at), with a fallback to the first drying stage_advance occurred_at (also client-supplied). assign_drying_station writes p_occurred_at verbatim as assigned_at; nothing bounds it to <= now() (the validator only checks ISO shape). Calling assign_drying_station(lot, station, now() - interval '60 days') instantly makes rest_days~60 and r_met true on a lot that started drying today. record_moisture_reading.occurred_at is likewise unbounded, so the moisture-stability half can be staged the same way. In this single-owner authenticated app this is the owner self-defeating their own gate rather than a tenant/privilege breach (hence MED, not CRIT), but it converts the 'enforced at the data layer, not on trust' guarantee the ROADMAP markets to buyers/auditors back into an honor system on a client clock, undermining the tamper-evidence the drying dossier is sold on.
findings.scenario: Owner under time pressure calls assign_drying_station('JC-602','st-bed-1', now() - interval '7 days') on a lot that landed on the bed today, records two in-band readings, and v_reposo_status reports ready - milling a lot that never rested. The append-only ledger now carries the back-dated assigned_at as 'evidence', so the dossier looks compliant.
findings.fix: Bound the field clock in the write door: in assign_drying_station and record_moisture_reading raise check_violation when p_occurred_at > now() + small_skew (and reject absurdly-old assignment times per business rule), mirrored in the command validators for a friendly pre-roundtrip error. Optionally anchor the rest-clock math to the server accept clock (recorded_at) instead of the client occurred_at, keeping occurred_at for display only, so a back-dated field time cannot shorten real elapsed rest.

verify_verdicts_so_far.reasoning:
VERIFIED on integrated main, but the finding's severity is inflated by an overstated failure narrative. Confirmed facts: (1) No test reads or exercises public/sw.js — grep across all *.test.* finds only the pure-function mirror (src/lib/offline/__tests__/sw-strategy.test.ts, which tests chooseStrategy/isPrecachable in sw-strategy.ts) and the registrar's register() assertion (sw-register.test.tsx). No fake CacheStorage exists anywhere in the suite. (2) The side-effecting handlers in public/sw.js — install→cache.addAll (sw.js:50-56), activate→old-cache purge `!k.startsWith(CACHE_VERSION)`+clients.claim (sw.js:58-71), and message→SKIP_WAITING (sw.js:138-140) — have ZERO behavioral coverage. (3) This code is reachable on prod today: NEXT_PUBLIC_OFFLINE defaults ON (src/lib/offline/flag.ts), and ServiceWorkerRegistrar is mounted in src/app/layout.tsx:50 and registers /sw.js, so these handlers run on real field devices. (4) The cache-version purge filter at sw.js:65 is CORRECT code today, so this is a missing-test on working code, not a latent bug.

Why I downgrade HIGH→MED (refuting the finding's headline): the "stale CACHE_VERSION → serves a stale chunk → white screen, the exact footgun" causal chain does NOT hold given the slice's own design. Static assets are content-hashed and cache-first, so a new build emits NEW filenames that miss-and-fetch from network — the old cache holds dead old-filename entries, not a stale-serve. The precached "/" shell is served stale-while-revalidate, so it refreshes behind on every navigation when online. The CACHE_VERSION purge is belt-and-suspenders cleanup, not the sole white-screen defense; a forgotten version bump leaves harmless dead caches, it does not straightforwardly blank-screen the crew. So the "release-discipline version-bump guard" half of the proposed fix pins a far lower-probability failure than claimed, and the CRIT-flavored "footgun unpinned" framing is not reachable as described.

What IS legitimately worth fixing (and arguably stronger than the version angle, same file/slice): the untested side-effecting handlers, and — most importantly — the MIRROR-SYNC invariant. sw-strategy.ts's own header declares "public/sw.js mirrors this logic... keep the two in sync; this file is the spec the test pins," yet isStaticAsset/isApiOrAuth/chooseStrategy are hand-duplicated in sw.js with NO test enforcing parity. Those two copies can silently drift (e.g. someone adds a route class to the SSOT and forgets sw.js), and the whole 48-test suite stays green. That is the live, reachable quality-gate gap. Net: REAL test-efficacy/missing-coverage gap on reachable production code (a CLAUDE.md branch-coverage + live-guard violation), but it is a missing test on currently-correct code — no bug, no data-corruption, no invariant bypass — so MED, not HIGH.
verify_verdicts_so_far.correctedFix:
Add a jsdom test (src/components/layout/__tests__/sw-lifecycle.test.ts or similar) that loads public/sw.js as text and drives its real handlers against a fake CacheStorage + fetch, asserting the BEHAVIOR rather than the literal version string: (1) install → the PRECACHE cache receives the PRECACHE_URLS via addAll; (2) activate → seed caches.open("janson-OLD-runtime") and "janson-v1-runtime", dispatch activate, assert every key NOT prefixed by the current CACHE_VERSION is deleted and the current-version keys survive (this pins the purge filter behaviorally, so a refactor that breaks `!k.startsWith(CACHE_VERSION)` fails red); (3) message "SKIP_WAITING" → self.skipWaiting() called. Load sw.js by reading the file and evaluating it with stubbed self/caches/fetch (e.g. new Function with injected globals), since it can't be imported as a module.

Most valuable addition — enforce the mirror-sync the SSOT header promises: a static test that requires BOTH public/sw.js and src/lib/offline/sw-strategy.ts to agree on the routing decision for a shared table of (method, url)→strategy cases (extract sw.js's chooseStrategy via the same eval harness and run the SAME case table the strategy unit test uses). This fails loudly the moment the two hand-duplicated copies drift — the real reachable regression vector — and is more important than version-bump pinning.

The version-bump "release-discipline guard" is OPTIONAL and lower-value given content-hashing + SWR; if added at all, derive CACHE_VERSION from the build hash / package.json version so it can't go stale rather than asserting a hand-edited bump.

verify_verdicts_so_far.reasoning:
VERIFIED as a real test-efficacy / guard gap, but DOWNGRADED HIGH→MED (not reachable as a bug today; latent).

Confirmed by reading the code:
- src/lib/offline/__tests__/sw-strategy.test.ts:3 imports ONLY `@/lib/offline/sw-strategy`. It never touches public/sw.js.
- public/sw.js:26-46 carries its own independent, inlined copy of isStaticAsset/isApiOrAuth/chooseStrategy. That file ships verbatim (static asset registered at /sw.js via src/components/layout/sw-register.tsx:25) and is NOT generated — no codegen step (only scripts/gen-seed.ts exists; package.json build is plain `next build`).
- No parity guard exists anywhere: grep for readFileSync/`public/sw`/`byte-identical`/`parity` across the repo returns nothing but the honor-system comments. sw-strategy.ts:8 literally says "Keep the two in sync; this file is the spec the test pins" and sw.js:9-12 says "keep the two in sync" — yet nothing enforces it.
- So the finding's structural claim is exactly right: the "never cache a write / never serve stale data" invariants are verified only on a twin module that the browser never executes. A maintainer can edit isApiOrAuth/isStaticAsset/chooseStrategy in sw-strategy.ts (test green) and forget public/sw.js, and the SW silently misroutes (e.g. a GET to an edge function served stale-while-revalidate) with the suite still green.

Why MED, not HIGH: I diffed the two strategy blocks — they are byte-identical today (modulo TS types/comments). There is NO shipped behavioral defect on main right now; the SW routes every case correctly. The risk is purely future drift on a hand-maintained duplicate. Per the rubric that is a guard/test-efficacy gap (MED: "a guard ... bug a user hits" only after a future edit), not a "real plausible-scenario bug" reachable today (HIGH) and certainly not a presently-bypassable data invariant (CRIT). The finding's own scenario is explicitly hypothetical ("a maintainer adds..."), confirming it is latent. The downgrade is the only correction; the defect itself is real and the fix is correct.

One nuance worth noting (does not change the verdict): even the scenario's specific example is partially self-protecting — the method gate (`!== GET → network-only`) runs first in BOTH copies, so a queued command POST stays network-only regardless of isApiOrAuth drift. The genuine exposure of a drift is GET-side misrouting (stale data / stale RSC document), as the finding also states. So the "caches a Supabase write" framing is slightly overstated for the write path but correct for the read/stale-data path.
verify_verdicts_so_far.correctedFix:
Add a parity guard test (lowest layer per the bug→regression-test rule) under src/lib/offline/__tests__/ that reads public/sw.js as text via fs.readFileSync and asserts its strategy logic agrees with the source of truth. Two viable forms:

(a) Behavioral parity (preferred, survives whitespace edits): extract the chooseStrategy/isStaticAsset/isApiOrAuth function bodies from the sw.js text (or `new Function(...)` them in a sandbox) and run the SAME table of url/method cases used in sw-strategy.test.ts through both the imported `chooseStrategy` and the sw.js copy, asserting identical results for every case (POST/PUT → network-only, /_next/static + favicon + manifest → cache-first, *.supabase.co + /rest/v1/ + /auth/ → network-first, /weigh + /harvests → stale-while-revalidate). This catches a one-sided edit to any of the three predicates.

(b) Source parity (cheaper, byte-level): strip TS type annotations + comments from sw-strategy.ts lines 26-51 and assert the normalized predicate bodies are substring-present in public/sw.js. More brittle (reformatting trips it) but trivial.

Better still — eliminate the duplication: emit the strategy block of public/sw.js from sw-strategy.ts at build via a small prebuild script (single source of truth), so there is nothing to keep in sync. Given the $0/no-CI constraint, the test-based guard (a) is the right minimal fix because it runs in `npm run test` (the local quality gate) with no infra.

verify_verdicts_so_far.reasoning:
PARTIALLY REAL — the coverage gap is real, but the HIGH severity rests on a refuted mechanism. Down-rated to MED.

CONFIRMED FACTS (read on disk):
- public/sw.js:16 hardcodes CACHE_VERSION = "janson-v1" (single commit d11f9e2, package version still 0.1.0 — never bumped).
- public/sw.js:57-71 activate handler purges caches via !k.startsWith(CACHE_VERSION); install precache (49-55) and message/SKIP_WAITING handler (138-140) also exist.
- NO test reads/imports/drives public/sw.js. The only SW tests are: (1) sw-strategy.test.ts, which tests src/lib/offline/sw-strategy.ts — a SEPARATE hand-maintained REIMPLEMENTATION of only the routing pure-functions, NOT the real SW file; and (2) sw-register.test.tsx, which only asserts register("/sw.js") is called. So the activate-purge, install precache, and SKIP_WAITING side-effecting handlers in the actual sw.js are 100% uncovered, and sw-strategy.ts can silently drift from sw.js (the code itself says "Keep the two in sync"). That part of the finding is accurate.

WHY I REFUTE THE HIGH-SEVERITY SCENARIO (the white-screen footgun): The finding claims a forgotten CACHE_VERSION bump → stale shell → blank screen. Tracing THIS code's actual browser mechanics, that does not happen:
  - _next/static chunks are content-hashed and served cache-first. A new build emits NEW filenames; new HTML requests them → cache MISS → network fetch. Old chunks are never served (they're just dead cache entries). No stale chunk reaches the page even with a stale CACHE_VERSION. This is exactly what sw-strategy.ts:13 documents ("a new build's new filenames simply miss-and-fetch").
  - The shell "/" is stale-while-revalidate: old shell paints first, but the old chunks it references are still cache-first present and work, while SWR refreshes "/" to new HTML behind it. Next load = new HTML + miss-fetched new chunks. No white screen.
  - The browser byte-compares sw.js per navigation; CACHE_VERSION only needs bumping on a breaking SW-cache-SCHEMA change, NOT every deploy. A stale "v1" across releases is the NORMAL/intended state. The header comment "bumped per release" is misleading, but the real stale-chunk defense is content-hashing + SWR, not the version string.
So the specific reachable-today disaster the finding asserts is not reachable; the version bump is not the load-bearing guard it's framed as.

NET: A genuine TEST-EFFICACY gap (real SW side-effects + the strategy mirror are untested, can drift green) — but it's a maintainability/coverage gap (MED), not a reachable-today HIGH bug, because the severity-justifying white-screen mechanism is refuted by the code's own content-hash + SWR design.
verify_verdicts_so_far.correctedFix: Add a node test (e.g. src/lib/offline/__tests__/sw-mirror.test.ts) that READS public/sw.js as text and pins the side-effecting contract the unit tests miss: (a) the activate handler deletes caches NOT prefixed by CACHE_VERSION (assert the filter `!k.startsWith(CACHE_VERSION)` / `caches.delete` are present); (b) PRECACHE_URLS contains exactly the static shell ("/", "/manifest.webmanifest", "/favicon.svg"); (c) the SKIP_WAITING message handler exists. Better: drive the real handlers against a fake CacheStorage (install→cache.addAll, activate→old-cache deleted) so behavior, not a string mirror, is exercised. To kill the drift hazard the code itself flags, add a guard test that extracts the isStaticAsset/isApiOrAuth/chooseStrategy logic from public/sw.js and asserts it matches src/lib/offline/sw-strategy.ts (so the two can't silently diverge). Do NOT add a "CACHE_VERSION must bump every release" guard — that mechanism is not the actual stale-chunk defense (content-hashed cache-first + SWR is) and such a guard would be a false constraint that fails CI on benign deploys. Separately, fix the misleading header comment in public/sw.js ("bumped per release") to reflect that the version only needs bumping on a breaking SW-cache-schema change.

Reuse the shared primitive instead of forking it. Two viable fixes, lowest-effort first:

Option A (preferred — kills the fork): render the outbox panel's contents as children of the shared <Dialog open onClose title="Sync activity"> from src/components/ui/dialog.tsx. Dialog already owns Escape-close, scroll-lock, initial focus, focus trap, and focus restore. Option B (if the slide-over layout must stay bespoke): port Dialog's focus management into OutboxDrawer verbatim — add a panelRef on the <aside>; on open, capture document.activeElement into a restoreRef and move focus to the first focusable inside the panel (or the panel itself, give it tabIndex={-1}); restore focus on unmount; set document.body.style.overflow="hidden" in a useEffect with a cleanup that resets it; and add the Tab/Shift+Tab wrap handler (copy dialog.tsx:77-102) to the portal root's onKeyDown alongside the existing Escape handler. Also move the Escape keydown to a document-level listener (as dialog.tsx:35-46 does) so Escape works before focus is inside the panel. Add tests asserting: focus lands inside the drawer on open, Tab from the last control wraps to the first, focus returns to the pill on close, and Escape closes from the initial (pill-focused) state.

verify_verdicts_so_far.reasoning:
REPRODUCED by reading the integrated code on main.

Structure (verified):
- src/components/layout/topbar.tsx:31 mounts <SyncStatus/>.
- src/components/layout/sync-status-island.tsx:33-47 — SyncStatus renders, as siblings under a fragment, the pill <SyncStatusPill onClick={()=>setOpen(true)}/> and, when open, <OutboxDrawer/>.
- The pill is a <button> defined in src/components/layout/sync-status.tsx:98-116 — a DOM/React child of the topbar, NOT of the drawer.
- OutboxDrawer (sync-status-island.tsx:62-215) createPortals to document.body. Its ONLY Escape handler is a React onKeyDown on the portal root <div role="dialog"> at lines 87-89; that div has no tabIndex and is never focused.

The crux — React synthetic event propagation — holds. Focus remains on the pill after click (the island has ZERO useRef/useEffect/.focus() — confirmed by grep; the only "focus" token in the file is the docstring at line 26). React bubbles a keydown up the React-ancestor chain of the focused node (the pill): pill -> SyncStatus fragment -> topbar -> root. OutboxDrawer is a SIBLING branch, never an ancestor of the pill in either the React tree or DOM, so its onKeyDown never fires. (React's portal special-casing bubbles events that ORIGINATE inside a portal up to React ancestors — it does not deliver a sibling-originated event into the portal.) Therefore Escape is dead until the user first clicks/Tabs onto a button inside the drawer (X / Retry / Dismiss), at which point focus is inside the portal subtree and the handler works. Exactly as the finding states.

No rescue exists: grep of layout/app shows the only document/window keydown listeners are command-palette.tsx:109 (Cmd-K) and mobile-nav.tsx:48 (its own Escape) — neither handles the sync drawer. The shared Dialog (src/components/ui/dialog.tsx:35-46) does it correctly via document.addEventListener('keydown'), and additionally moves focus into the panel (54-72) and traps Tab (77-102) — none of which the drawer replicates.

Contract violation is explicit: the docstring at sync-status-island.tsx:26-27 advertises a "focus-trapped, Escape-closable dialog." Neither is true — focus is never moved in (so no trap) and Escape is dead. The dedicated test file (src/components/layout/__tests__/sync-status-island.test.tsx) only asserts the pill renders and the drawer opens on click; it never presses Escape, so the broken behavior ships untested (a false-green gap).

Severity MED is right: keyboard/touch UX + a11y defect a user hits immediately (opens drawer, hits Escape, nothing), not data corruption/security/money. The backdrop click and X still close it, so it is degraded, not a hard trap. Reachable on integrated main today.

One nuance vs the finding's wording: the finding says the handler is dead "until the user has manually clicked a button inside the drawer." Slightly more precise — it is dead until focus lands ANYWHERE inside the portal subtree (click OR Tab into the X/Retry/Dismiss). Doesn't change the verdict.
